LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT SPRINGPOINT SENIOR LIVING, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 17, 2011 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 301 TO BE HEARD WITH HUDSON CITY BANCORP'S MOTION TO DISMISS. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT MR. ESCHER WHOSE NAME APPEAR ON THE PLEADING IN DEFENDANT'S SPRINGOPOINT WORKED WITH HIM 11 YEARS AGO IN THE SAME LAW FIRM BEFORE HIS APPOINTMENT TO THE BENCH AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW THIS AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. SPECIALLY-APPEARING DEFENDANT HUDSON CITY BANCORP, INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. THE ALLEGED ACT GIVING RISE TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OCCURED, IF AT ALL, SOLELY IN NEW JERSEY, THE CAUSE OF ACTION DID NOT ARISE IN CALIFORNIA. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, KENT S. GUBBINE CSR # 5797 301/PJB

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DENIS SALAMONE'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FORUM NON CONVENIENS. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. "WHEN A NONRESIDENT DEFENDANT CHALLENGES PERSONAL JURISDICTION, THE PLAINTIFF MUST PROVE, BY A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE, THE FACTUAL BASIS THAT WOULD JUSTIFY THE EXERCISE OF JURISDICTION." DOREL INDUSTRIES, INC. V. SUP. CRT. (2005) 134 CAL.APP.4TH 1267, 1273. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT SALAMONE HAD ANY CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA TO WARRANT THE EXERCISE OF GENERAL OR SPECIFIC JURISDICTION OVER HIM. THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO CONDUCT JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189.

LAW AND MOTION 302, AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ON SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS SPRINGPOINT AT MONROE, INC. (FORMERLY KNOWN AS THE PRESBYTERIAN HOME AT MONROE, INC.), TED ALTER, SUSAN WOOD, AND REBECCA FELLER'S MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS AND DISMISS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS GRANTED. "WHEN A NONRESIDENT DEFENDANT CHALLENGES PERSONAL JURISDICTION, THE PLAINTIFF MUST PROVE, BY A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE, THE FACTUAL BASIS THAT WOULD JUSTIFY THE EXERCISE OF JURISDICTION." DOREL INDUSTRIES, INC. V. SUP. CRT. (2005) 134 CAL.APP.4TH 1267, 1273. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANTS (SPRINGPOINT AT MONROE, INC. TED ALTER, SUSAN WOOD, AND REBECCA FELLER) HAD ANY CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA TO WARRANT THE EXERCISE OF GENERAL OR SPECIFIC JURISDICTION OVER THEM. THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO CONDUCT JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY. IN ADDITION, THE CASE MUST BE DISMISSED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SERVE DEFENDANTS IN A TIMELY FASHION. SEE CCP 583.210 AND 583.250. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189.

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction, Or In The Alternative, Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lincoln Bandlow, Esq. of Lathrop & Gage, LLP, Tel. 310.789.4604, for defendants/moving party;
Jordan Rosenberg, Tel. 206.666.6517, plaintiff and opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT DENIS SALAMONE'S Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction, Or In The Alternative, Forum Non Conveniens. The motion to quash is granted. "When a nonresident defendant challenges personal jurisdiction, the plaintiff must prove, by a preponderance of the evidence, the factual basis that would justify the exercise of jurisdiction." Dorel Industries, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1267, 1273. Plaintiff fails to show that defendant Salamone had any contacts with California to warrant the exercise of general or specific jurisdiction over him. The court denies plaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189.

Ntc Of Mtn Of Specially Appearing Springpoint Deft'S Mtn To Quash Summons And Dismiss First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kate A. Wolf, Esq. of Shook Hardy & Bacon LLP, Tel. 415.544.1900, for defendants/moving party;
Jordan Rosenberg, Tel. 206.666.6517, plaintiff and opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on Specially Appearing Defendants SPRINGPOINT AT MONROE, INC. (FORMERLY KNOWN AS THE PRESBYTERIAN HOME AT MONROE, INC.), TED ALTER, SUSAN WOOD, and REBECCA FELLER'S Motion To Quash Summons And Dismiss First Amended Complaint. The motion to quash is granted. "When a nonresident defendant challenges personal jurisdiction, the plaintiff must prove, by a preponderance of the evidence, the factual basis that would justify the exercise of jurisdiction." Dorel Industries, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1267, 1273. Plaintiff fails to show that defendants (Springpoint at Monroe, Inc. Ted Alter, Susan Wood, and Rebecca Feller) had any contacts with California to warrant the exercise of general or specific jurisdiction over them. The court denies plaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery. In addition, the case must be dismissed because plaintiff failed to serve defendants in a timely fashion. See CCP 583.210 and 583.250. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189.
